title: Regression Test Plan — Upstream Failure Pattern Coverage
source: Upstream anomalyco/opencode open issues mapped to derivative architecture

upstream_issues: MCP tab showing MCP not configured
domains.risk: UI projection lies about runtime truth
domains.invariant: If an MCP server is configured, connected, failed, disabled, quarantined, or loading, the UI shows the correct state and cites the runtime source. Desktop must not derive MCP state from a different config path than the agent/tool registry.

domains.risk: MCP config disappears when project .opencode/ exists
domains.invariant: Config resolver: global + project + workspace + plugin-origin + runtime overrides. MCP, providers, plugins, and hooks resolve through the same precedence chain. No domain has its own resolver.

domains.domain: Safe mode — renderer crash before UI
domains.risk: Status widget crash kills entire app before debug logs available
domains.invariant: Any server/MCP/plugin status rendering tolerates malformed, partial, disabled, stale, or unknown entries. UI renders 'malformed server entry' with debug export option. Never crashes before logs exportable.

domains.domain: MCP process deduplication and listener cleanup
domains.risk: Duplicate MCP processes (24 node processes, 1.8GB RAM), stream crashes, listener leaks
domains.invariant: One MCP process per intended scope. No duplicate starts per session. Listener cleanup on disconnect, restart, project close, plugin uninstall, app shutdown. Stream close idempotency: writing after close becomes typed terminal event.

domains.risk: Heap grows to 1-2GB under sustained use
domains.invariant: Event payload caps enforced. Debug packets bound. Listener cleanup on disconnect. Sidecar watchdog terminates hung processes. Session timelines not fully loaded into UI state. Long-run soak: multiple sessions, MCP servers, streaming responses, plugin hooks, event inspector open — verify listener counts, process counts, heap growth, watcher cleanup.

domains.risk: Malformed tool calls persist as pending forever; infinite loops of empty assistant messages
domains.invariant: Every tool call reaches exactly one terminal state. Schema validation failure settles tool part to error. No pending part survives without active executor. Empty assistant/model responses have loop breakers. Token-zero cycles detected and stopped with recoverable event.

domains.domain: Loop breaker — zero-token / empty assistant cycles
domains.risk: Infinite loop after tool call with empty text
domains.invariant: Max consecutive empty responses cap. Token-zero detection. Recoverable event emitted on loop break. Session not permanently stuck.

domains.risk: GET /session/status no longer aggregates across child directories
domains.invariant: Campaign runtime exposes global status: all active campaigns, lanes, tool calls, claims, busy sessions — regardless of workspace subdirectory. Orchestrator has a global truth surface.

domains.risk: Generic OpenAI-compatible path assumes providers support response_format, prefill, vision, cache-control, reasoning-effort
domains.invariant: Provider adapters negotiate capabilities. Model-specific request shaping. Safe fallback on unsupported features. Typed 'provider does not support this modality' error for unsupported inputs.

domains.risk: Image attachments silently degraded through custom providers
domains.invariant: File parts delivered as valid vision input or rejected with typed error. Provider-specific translation tested per modality.

domains.risk: claims_acquired fake-positive, redteam event mismatch, critic verdict loss, validation freshness, init() cold-start gap
domains.invariant: All predicates require positive evidence. Event names consistent across emit/consume. Critic preserves verdict. Binder evidence reconstructable from events. Lane can reconstruct from durable store without pre-existing memory.
